title: Daily Market Digest - June 22, 2026
subtitle: A professional overview of today's market movements and key financial news. Not financial advice.

items.headline: U.S. Equities Mixed as Investors Weigh Geopolitical Progress and Fed Outlook
items.summary: U.S. stock markets are mixed to start the week on Monday, June 22, 2026, as Wall Street assesses progress in U.S.-Iran peace talks, which eased oil prices and inflation concerns, while also bracing for upcoming economic data and expectations of potential Fed rate hikes. Tech and discretionary stocks were strong performers in the prior trading session, but the Nasdaq saw some weakness today. The CBOE Volatility Index (VIX) decreased 9% to 16.78.

items.headline: Apogee Therapeutics Soars on Acquisition News
items.summary: Apogee Therapeutics ($APGE) soared 47% in premarket trading following an announcement that AbbVie ($ABBV) is acquiring the immunology drug developer in a $10.9 billion all-cash deal.

items.headline: Intel Jumps as Semiconductor Rally Continues
items.summary: Intel Corporation ($INTC) surged 10.6% to a record high, sparking gains across the semiconductor industry amid optimism for AI demand and future investment in chip technologies.

items.headline: Micron Technology Gains Ahead of Earnings
items.summary: Micron Technology ($MU) gained 4.3% in premarket trading and closed +8.7% on Thursday, ahead of its closely watched earnings report scheduled for Wednesday. Analysts raised price targets citing strong demand for DRAM fueled by AI infrastructure.

items.headline: SpaceX Slips for Third Straight Day Post-IPO
items.summary: Elon Musk's SpaceX ($SPCX) is heading for its third straight day of losses, falling nearly 5% to $175.96 in premarket trading as post-debut volatility continues to grip the stock following its blockbuster IPO on June 12.

items.headline: Alamos Gold Tumbles in Premarket Trade
items.summary: Alamos Gold ($AGI) tumbled 12.3% in premarket trading. This move comes as weakness continues in rate-sensitive sectors.

items.headline: Technology and Consumer Discretionary Lead, Energy Lags Amid AI Optimism and Geopolitical Shifts
items.summary: The Technology Select Sector SPDR (XLK) advanced 2.7%, followed by the Consumer Discretionary Select Sector SPDR (XLY) at 1.8%, and Communication Services Select Sector SPDR (XLC) at 1.1%. Information Technology continues to top the S&P 500, fueled by optimism around AI demand, notably in semiconductors. Conversely, the Energy Select Sector SPDR (XLE) declined 1.7% and remains a laggard, reflecting easing oil prices and geopolitical developments. Communications and Health Care sectors are also lagging.

items.headline: Nasdaq-100 and S&P 500 Undergo Quarterly Rebalance
items.summary: Effective prior to today's market open, the Nasdaq-100 added five companies, largely AI-related, including Astera Labs ($ALAB), CoreWeave ($CRWV), Nebius Group ($NBIS), Rocket Lab ($RKLB), and Teradyne ($TER). It removed Charter Communications ($CHTR), Cognizant ($CTSH), Insmed ($INSM), Verisk Analytics ($VRSK), and Zscaler ($ZS). The S&P 500 added Marvell Technology ($MRVL) and Flex ($FLEX), replacing Pool Corp ($POOL) and The Campbell's Company ($CPB).

items.headline: Fed Holds Rates Steady, Signals Hawkish Stance Amid Elevated Inflation Concerns
items.summary: The Federal Reserve kept interest rates unchanged at its June meeting. However, under new Chair Kevin Warsh, the statement adopted a hawkish tone, focusing on price stability and dropping previous easing bias language. The updated 'dot plot' now projects at least one rate hike by year-end 2026 by several participants, with bond futures pricing in over 85% odds for a hike. The Fed's year-end PCE inflation forecast was significantly raised to 3.6% from 2.7%.

items.headline: U.S.-Iran Peace Agreement Drives Down Oil Prices, Eases Inflation Worries
items.summary: Investor sentiment improved following reports of significant progress in U.S.-Iran peace negotiations over the weekend, leading to an easing of geopolitical tensions and the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz. This has resulted in a notable decline in crude oil prices, with Brent crude falling to $79.02 per barrel, down 2%, and U.S. crude falling to $75.11. This development is alleviating some inflation concerns.

items.headline: Key Economic Data Releases Expected This Week
items.summary: The macroeconomic schedule for the week includes the S&P Global Flash PMIs for June on Tuesday, May new home sales on Wednesday, and critical May personal income and spending data, alongside the Federal Reserve's preferred inflation gauge, the Personal Consumption Expenditures (PCE) price index, on Thursday. Economists anticipate the May consumer inflation measure to accelerate to 4.1% from 3.8% in April.

items.headline: Micron Technology Among Companies Reporting Earnings This Week
items.summary: While several smaller companies are reporting earnings today, major focus this week will be on Micron Technology ($MU), which is scheduled to release its earnings on Wednesday, June 24. Other significant reports expected this week include Carnival ($CCL), FedEx ($FDX), BlackBerry ($BB), and Nano-X Imaging ($NNOX).
